same easy fee baseline | edit the corresponding all-sheet first
same easy base profile | edit the corresponding all-sheet first
kept at nice-length; jolly variance comes from per-wave low/nice/big/mega mix, not from longer runs
use easy baseline wave length; jolly unpredictability comes from mixed sub-wave profiles
same as difficulty low until retuned
same as difficulty low until retuned
same as difficulty low until retuned
weighted expected triple chance from low/nice/big/mega easy mix
kept aligned to low/nice/big; only mega differs here
kept at standard easy special timing until explicit jolly per-wave timing is tuned
held at the stable non-mega cap; jolly should be unpredictable, not just richer
same as difficulty low until retuned
same as difficulty low until retuned
kept aligned to low/nice/big; mega remains the obvious reward tier
kept at standard easy quick timing until jolly-specific wave mix is tuned
per_game weighted expected prize rate from low/nice/big/mega easy mix
score only; absolute prize tuned separately
kept at standard easy prize timing until jolly-specific wave mix is tuned
kept at standard easy timing; mix variance should come from wave profile selection
kept at standard easy bomb timing; difficulty should come from composition, not hidden speed-up

primary manual edit sheet for hard; sync sheets should mirror these values
Edit only all easy, all medium, all hard
These are the primary manual edit surfaces.
Do not change parameter, unit, or note columns
Only change the numeric cells under low, nice, big, mega, jolly.
Treat the * sync sheets as mirrors
After manual edits, ask Codex to resync them.
Update shared PPP and BF/USDC in the data sheet first
Then keep the per-sheet reference rows aligned when resyncing.
Use percentage formulas in all sheets if useful
Current default ladder is low baseline, then -10% over low->nice->big->mega.
Jolly is a temporary placeholder column
It can stay neutral until the per-wave jolly design is tuned.
Average is removed from the active ladder
Do not recreate it unless we explicitly decide to restore it.
Use extra_waves to control type progression
total_waves should stay coherent with base_waves + extra_waves.
Use bombs_base_per_wave and bombs_second_chance to change variance
This is one of the strongest levers on profitable-run frequency.
Use triple and quick rows to move value from stable to spiky outcomes
Higher special value usually lowers profitable-run frequency while preserving RTP better.
Change a few numbers, then ask for resync + Monte Carlo
Do not redesign many branches at once if you want readable comparisons.
Keep manual tuning focused on one difficulty branch at a time
We are currently preparing the maxi-change model in ltm2sync locally.

shared candidate PPP from data sheet
shared reference snapshot from data sheet
update this first when testing a new shared PPP
shared reference snapshot for all sync sheets
refresh when BF/USDC is refetched live
kept explicit so the snapshot time is unambiguous
-10% over low->nice->big->mega; jolly keeps base easy timings and gets variance from mixed sub-wave profiles
for wave and butterfly durations, each game type is 10% faster than the previous type within the same difficulty branch
Update shared values here first. Active type ladder is low, nice, big, mega; jolly uses its own expected-mix profile.
single source of truth for shared tuning inputs
all easy / all medium / all hard
edit these aggregate sheets first; treat sync sheets as generated mirrors
after manual edits in all sheets, Codex can resync the per-type sheets from them
